answer:
**Charles Comte, in *Traité de Législation Vol. II*, Chapter 10, examines the intellectual development of island peoples in the Pacific ("great Ocean"), primarily of Malay and negro/Ethiopian species, across latitudes from the tropics southward.** He specifically discusses the following islands or archipelagos, often comparing their agriculture, dwellings, pirogues, social organization, and curiosity relative to latitude and climate:[^1]

- **Marquesas Islands** (tropical, Malay species; noted for agriculture, property division, and industry).[^2]
- **Navigators’ Islands** (Samoa; 13°S; praised for clean villages and houses).[^3]
- **Society Islands** (tropical; advanced pirogues, agriculture, navigation, and calculation).[^4]
- **Friendly Islands** (Tonga; divided lands, roads, hedges).[^5]
- **Sandwich Islands** (Hawaii; agriculture adapted to less fertile soil, curiosity toward Europeans).[^6]
- **Easter Island** (8° farther south; rudimentary tools but intelligent cultivation).[^7]
- **New Zealand** (20°S; pirogues and fortifications but filthy habits, low curiosity).[^8]
- **Van Diemen's Land** (Tasmania; negro species; extreme primitiveness, no agriculture).[^9]
- **New Holland** (Australia; slightly more advanced than Tasmania, with chiefs and basic fishing/hunting).[^10]
- **New Caledonia** (negro species; advanced agriculture with walls and irrigation).[^11]
- **Tanna** (Vanuatu; agriculture and fishing despite crude tools).[^12]
- **New Hebrides** (Vanuatu; fast canoes).[^13]
- **New Guinea** (negroes; mats, pottery, trade with Chinese).[^14]

He briefly references but declines to discuss the Sunda Islands, Philippines, and Moluccas due to mixed populations.[^15]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [ch.10](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/).

[^2]: Ibid., [p-7bc4d8](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-7bc4d8).

[^3]: Ibid., [p-dd9c59](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-dd9c59).

[^4]: Ibid., [p-5cad8f](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-5cad8f).

[^5]: Ibid., [p-d31774](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-d31774).

[^6]: Ibid.

[^7]: Ibid., [p-e3f8c0](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-e3f8c0).

[^8]: Ibid., [p-a1b11f](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-a1b11f).

[^9]: Ibid., [p-c9c904](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-c9c904).

[^10]: Ibid., [p-5778c4](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-5778c4).

[^11]: Ibid., [p-6cb12b](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-6cb12b).

[^12]: Ibid., [p-203655](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-203655).

[^13]: Ibid., [p-bcfe9d](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-bcfe9d).

[^14]: Ibid., [p-fbe0c3](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-fbe0c3).

[^15]: Ibid., [p-7b89c4](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/10-du-developpement-intellectuel-acquis-dans-les-iles/translation/#p-7b89c4).
